label: Structure
summary: Classes, functions, state, and responsibility boundaries.

rules.label: Deep Inheritance Hierarchy
rules.description: Repository-local Python class chains with unusually deep inheritance depth.
rules.explanation: In Python, Deep Inheritance Hierarchy matters because the pattern here, Repository-local Python class chains with unusually deep inheritance depth, concentrates too much responsibility into one shape and makes future change riskier.
rules.fix: For Deep Inheritance Hierarchy in Python, split the class or constructor work so one object owns one role and collaborators are injected or composed deliberately.

rules.label: Eager Constructor Collaborators
rules.description: Constructors that instantiate several collaborators eagerly inside __init__.
rules.explanation: In Python, Eager Constructor Collaborators matters because the pattern here, Constructors that instantiate several collaborators eagerly inside __init__, concentrates too much responsibility into one shape and makes future change riskier.
rules.fix: For Eager Constructor Collaborators in Python, split the class or constructor work so one object owns one role and collaborators are injected or composed deliberately.

rules.label: God Class
rules.description: Python classes that concentrate unusually high method count, public surface area, and mutable instance state.
rules.explanation: In Python, God Class matters because the pattern here, Python classes that concentrate unusually high method count, public surface area, and mutable instance state, concentrates too much responsibility into one shape and makes future change riskier.
rules.fix: For God Class in Python, split the class or constructor work so one object owns one role and collaborators are injected or composed deliberately.

rules.label: God Function
rules.description: Very large Python functions with high control-flow and call-surface concentration.
rules.explanation: In Python, God Function matters because the pattern here, Very large Python functions with high control-flow and call-surface concentration, is worth review because oversized classes and mixed-concern functions hide the real domain seams of the codebase.
rules.fix: For God Function in Python, split responsibilities around the real domain boundary, and keep stateful objects narrow enough that their role is obvious.

rules.label: Mixed Concerns Function
rules.description: Functions that mix HTTP, persistence, and filesystem-style concerns in one body.
rules.explanation: In Python, Mixed Concerns Function matters because the pattern here, Functions that mix HTTP, persistence, and filesystem-style concerns in one body, makes the API contract harder to scan and easier to misuse.
rules.fix: For Mixed Concerns Function in Python, reshape the signature or call contract so the expected parameter order, ownership, or return shape is obvious at the definition site.

rules.label: Monolithic Init Module
rules.description: __init__.py files that carry enough imports and behavior to look like monolithic modules.
rules.explanation: In Python, Monolithic Init Module matters because the pattern here, __init__.py files that carry enough imports and behavior to look like monolithic modules, makes ownership and navigation harder when someone has to trace behavior across modules or services.
rules.fix: For Monolithic Init Module in Python, move the code to the package, module, router, or entrypoint layer that already owns this boundary so navigation and responsibility stay aligned.

rules.label: Monolithic Module
rules.description: Non-__init__.py modules that are unusually large and combine many imports with orchestration-heavy behavior.
rules.explanation: In Python, Monolithic Module matters because the pattern here, Non-__init__.py modules that are unusually large and combine many imports with orchestration-heavy behavior, makes ownership and navigation harder when someone has to trace behavior across modules or services.
rules.fix: For Monolithic Module in Python, move the code to the package, module, router, or entrypoint layer that already owns this boundary so navigation and responsibility stay aligned.

rules.label: Name Responsibility Mismatch
rules.description: Read-style, transformation-style, or utility-style names that still perform mutation or own multiple infrastructure concerns.
rules.explanation: In Python, Name Responsibility Mismatch matters because the pattern here, Read-style, transformation-style, or utility-style names that still perform mutation or own multiple infrastructure concerns, hides the real job of the symbol behind wording that is harder to scan during review.
rules.fix: For Name Responsibility Mismatch in Python, rename the symbol so its domain job is obvious without reading the whole body.

rules.label: Over Abstracted Wrapper
rules.description: Ceremonial wrapper-style or tiny data-container classes that add little beyond storing constructor state.
rules.explanation: In Python, Over Abstracted Wrapper matters because the pattern here, Ceremonial wrapper-style or tiny data-container classes that add little beyond storing constructor state, concentrates too much responsibility into one shape and makes future change riskier.
rules.fix: For Over Abstracted Wrapper in Python, split the class or constructor work so one object owns one role and collaborators are injected or composed deliberately.

rules.label: Tight Module Coupling
rules.description: Modules that depend on a large number of repository-local Python modules.
rules.explanation: In Python, Tight Module Coupling matters because the pattern here, Modules that depend on a large number of repository-local Python modules, makes ownership and navigation harder when someone has to trace behavior across modules or services.
rules.fix: For Tight Module Coupling in Python, move the code to the package, module, router, or entrypoint layer that already owns this boundary so navigation and responsibility stay aligned.

rules.label: Too Many Instance Attributes
rules.description: Classes that assign an unusually large number of instance attributes across their methods.
rules.explanation: In Python, Too Many Instance Attributes matters because the pattern here, Classes that assign an unusually large number of instance attributes across their methods, is worth review because oversized classes and mixed-concern functions hide the real domain seams of the codebase.
rules.fix: For Too Many Instance Attributes in Python, split responsibilities around the real domain boundary, and keep stateful objects narrow enough that their role is obvious.
